assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can receive email when the selected route requires it.
No platform account, tenant, credentials, or product resources exist yet.
Developer has access to Infobip API key

developer_goal: Integrate Infobip messaging capabilities into existing systems via API.

first_success.outcome: Developer sends a valid SMS message through Infobip API and receives a successful response
first_success.observable_signal: API response indicating message accepted and queued for delivery

route_selection.rule: Reconstruct complete official candidate routes, prefer no payment/admin/approval when possible, then minimize required actions; leave consequential ties unresolved.

candidates.name: Infobip Exchange Apps
candidates.reason: Exchange apps are for extending Infobip and integrating with new platforms but require prior API knowledge and account setup; the REST API is the fundamental integration surface.

candidates.name: Answers Web Interface
candidates.reason: The Answers web interface is for building chatbots via UI, suitable for chatbot designers rather than developers integrating via API.

prerequisites.description: Developer must have an active Infobip account to access API keys and services.
Developer must have an API key assigned at signup to authenticate API requests.
Developer should be familiar with REST API usage and have basic programming skills to integrate the API.

external_gates.description: Must have an active Infobip account to access API keys.
API key is required for authenticating API requests.

platform_outcomes.description: Successfully send a test SMS message using the Infobip REST API

uncertainties.question: Has the machine-drafted Infobip route been independently shortest-path audited?
uncertainties.impact: Action counts and peer comparison must stay withheld until a human audit passes.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Complete a shortest-path audit against current official docs and mark verified only when evidence supports it.
